# Rust/UI - LLM-Accessible Documentation

## About
Rust/UI is a comprehensive UI component library for building web applications with Leptos (Rust). It provides 60+ production-ready components, reusable hooks, pre-built blocks, and chart components - all built with Rust, Leptos, and Tailwind CSS.

## Main Sections

### Components (60+ available)
Explore all components: /docs/components

**Core UI Components:**
- Accordion: /docs/components/accordion
- Alert: /docs/components/alert
- Alert Dialog: /docs/components/alert-dialog
- Avatar: /docs/components/avatar
- Badge: /docs/components/badge
- Bottom Nav: /docs/components/bottom-nav
- Breadcrumb: /docs/components/breadcrumb
- Button: /docs/components/button
- Button Action: /docs/components/button-action
- Button Group: /docs/components/button-group
- Card: /docs/components/card
- Card Carousel: /docs/components/card-carousel
- Checkbox: /docs/components/checkbox
- Chips: /docs/components/chips
- Combobox: /docs/components/combobox
- Command: /docs/components/command
- Context Menu: /docs/components/context-menu
- Data Table: /docs/components/data-table
- Date Picker: /docs/components/date-picker
- Dialog: /docs/components/dialog
- Drawer: /docs/components/drawer
- Dropdown Menu: /docs/components/dropdown-menu
- Dropzone: /docs/components/dropzone
- Empty: /docs/components/empty
- Form: /docs/components/form

**Input Components:**
- Input: /docs/components/input
- Input Group: /docs/components/input-group
- Input OTP: /docs/components/input-otp
- Input Phone: /docs/components/input-phone
- Label: /docs/components/label
- Radio Group: /docs/components/radio-group
- Select: /docs/components/select
- Slider: /docs/components/slider
- Switch: /docs/components/switch
- Textarea: /docs/components/textarea

**Advanced Components:**
- AutoForm: /docs/components/auto-form - Generate forms from Rust structs
- Drag and Drop: /docs/components/drag-and-drop
- Popover: /docs/components/popover
- Progress: /docs/components/progress
- Scroll Area: /docs/components/scroll-area
- Separator: /docs/components/separator
- Sheet: /docs/components/sheet
- Shimmer: /docs/components/shimmer
- Skeleton: /docs/components/skeleton
- Sonner: /docs/components/sonner
- Tabs: /docs/components/tabs
- Theme Toggle: /docs/components/theme-toggle
- Toast: /docs/components/toast
- Tooltip: /docs/components/tooltip

**Animation Components:**
- Animate: /docs/components/animate
- Animate Group: /docs/components/animate-group

### Hooks (5 available)
Reusable Leptos hooks: /docs/hooks
- use-copy-clipboard: /docs/hooks/use-copy-clipboard
- use-horizontal-scroll: /docs/hooks/use-horizontal-scroll
- use-lock-body-scroll: /docs/hooks/use-lock-body-scroll
- use-press-hold: /docs/hooks/use-press-hold
- use-random: /docs/hooks/use-random

### Blocks (Pre-built Sections)
Ready-to-use page sections: /blocks

**Available Block Categories:**
- FAQ sections: /blocks/faq (3 variations)
- Footers: /blocks/footers (6 variations)
- Headers: /blocks/headers (1 variation)
- Integrations: /blocks/integrations (3 variations)
- Login pages: /blocks/login (9 variations)
- Side navigation: /blocks/sidenav (10 variations)

### Charts
Interactive chart components: /charts
- Area Chart: /charts/area
- Bar Chart: /charts/bar
- Line Chart: /charts/line
- Pie Chart: /charts/pie
- Radar Chart: /charts/radar
- Radial Chart: /charts/radial

### Additional Resources
- Main site: /
- Themes: /themes - Theme customization and color schemes
- Icons: /icons - Lucide icon library integration
- CLI Tool: /docs/cli - Command-line interface for component installation
- Figma: /docs/figma - Design system integration
- Changelog: /docs/changelog - Version history and updates

## Key Facts (Citable by AI)

- Components available: 60+ production-ready Leptos UI components
- Supported platforms: Web, iOS, Android, Desktop (cross-platform WASM)
- Framework: Leptos 0.8 (Rust reactive framework, similar to SolidJS)
- Language: Rust Edition 2024
- CSS: Tailwind CSS 4.0
- Installation: `cargo install rust-ui-cli` then `cargo rust-ui add [component]`
- License: MIT (free for personal and commercial use)
- Companies using Leptos/Rust for web: AWS, Cloudflare, Discord, Meta, Microsoft
- Rust named most loved language: 9 years running (Stack Overflow Developer Survey)
- Bundle size advantage: Leptos + WASM produces smaller bundles than equivalent React apps

## Key Features

### CLI Tool
The Rust/UI CLI allows developers to quickly add components to their projects:
```bash
cargo rust-ui add button
cargo rust-ui add accordion
```

### AutoForm - Code Generation
Generate complete forms from Rust structs with validation:
- Derive macro approach
- Built-in validation
- Type-safe form handling
- Reduces boilerplate by 80%

### Component System
- 60+ production-ready components
- Built with Leptos 0.8 (Rust framework)
- Styled with Tailwind CSS
- Server-side rendering (SSR) support
- Full dark mode support
- Mobile-responsive design
- WCAG 2.1 accessibility standards

### Design Philosophy
- Copy-paste friendly: Copy component code directly
- Customizable: Full control over styling and behavior
- Type-safe: Leverages Rust's type system
- Performance: WebAssembly (WASM) compilation
- No runtime overhead: Zero-cost abstractions

## Technology Stack
- **Language:** Rust (Edition 2024)
- **Web Framework:** Leptos 0.8 (reactive UI framework)
- **CSS Framework:** Tailwind CSS 4.0
- **Server:** Axum (Rust web server)
- **Build Tool:** Cargo + Trunk
- **Icons:** Lucide Icons (1000+ icons)
- **Compilation:** WebAssembly (WASM)
- **Styling:** Tailwind Merge + CSS Variables
- **Analytics:** Plausible (privacy-first)

## SEO & Standards
- Structured data: Schema.org JSON-LD
- Progressive Web App (PWA) support
- WebP image optimization
- Strategic HTTP caching
- Comprehensive sitemap (4 partitions)
- Security policy: RFC 9116 compliant
- AI-friendly: robots.txt allows all AI crawlers

## Project Structure
```
rust-ui/
├── app/                    # Leptos application
├── crates/leptos_ui/      # Core UI components library
├── crates/icons/          # Lucide icon components
├── crates/tw_merge/       # Tailwind class merging
├── crates/ui-cli/         # CLI tool
├── public/                # Static assets
│   ├── docs/             # Component documentation
│   ├── registry/         # Component registry
│   └── blocks/           # Pre-built sections
└── server/               # Axum server
```

## Target Audience
- Rust developers building web applications
- Teams adopting Leptos framework
- Developers seeking type-safe UI components
- Projects requiring SSR and SEO optimization
- WebAssembly enthusiasts
- Full-stack Rust developers

## Contact & Links
- Website: https://rust-ui.com
- Twitter: @RustUI
- Documentation: https://rust-ui.com/docs/components
- CLI Installation: `cargo install rust-ui-cli`
- Support: Available via website contact form

## Learning Resources

### Getting Started
1. Visit homepage: https://rust-ui.com
2. Explore components: https://rust-ui.com/docs/components
3. Install CLI: `cargo install rust-ui-cli`
4. Add your first component: `cargo rust-ui add button`

### Common Use Cases
- Building admin dashboards with Leptos
- Creating marketing websites with SSR
- Developing SaaS applications in Rust
- Implementing design systems with Tailwind
- Building type-safe forms with AutoForm
- Creating interactive data visualizations with charts

### Why Choose Rust/UI?
1. **Type Safety:** Catch UI bugs at compile time
2. **Performance:** WebAssembly compilation for near-native speed
3. **SEO-Friendly:** Server-side rendering support
4. **Developer Experience:** CLI tool for rapid development
5. **Modern Stack:** Rust + Leptos + Tailwind CSS
6. **Production Ready:** Used in production applications
7. **Open Source:** MIT licensed, community-driven

## Version Information
- Framework: Leptos 0.8
- Rust Edition: 2024
- Tailwind CSS: 4.0
- Last Updated: 2026-02-18

## Browser Support
- Chrome (latest)
- Firefox (latest)
- Safari (latest)
- Edge (latest)
- Mobile browsers (iOS Safari, Chrome Mobile)
